articles.title: Israel bans Jerusalem Grand Mufti from Al-Aqsa Mosque for a week
articles.summary: Israel bans Jerusalem Grand Mufti from Al-Aqsa Mosque for a week Israeli forces first detained Sheikh Mohammad Hussein, the Grand Mufti of Jerusalem and Palestine and preacher of Al-Aqsa Mosque, after he concluded Friday sermon and prayers, WAFA reported. Following his detention, Israeli authorities handed Sheikh Hussein an order barring him from entering the Al-Aqsa Mosque complex for one week. In August last year, Israeli authorities issued a six-month ban on Sheikh Hussein, after he delivered a Friday sermon condemning Israel’s starvation policy against Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.&

articles.title: Report: Traffic in Hormuz falls sharply, only six vessels passed on 9-10 July night
articles.summary: Report: Traffic in Hormuz falls sharply, only six vessels passed on 9-10 July night Ship-tracking firm Windward said just six vessels crossed the Strait of Hormuz on the night of 9-10 July, compared with 18 to 22 a few days ago. This was the third night of reduced traffic in the critical waterway, where Iranian attacks on ships led to a renewed wave of US strikes and Iranian counterattacks on the neighbouring Gulf nations. Outbound sailings have nearly stopped, just one vessel last night, against 15-21 on 5-7 July, Windward said in a post on X. Windward said “dark transits”, where vessels turn

Another Israeli strike targeted a vehicle in Nabatieh town, injuring two civilians.

articles.title: Third UNSC meet on Iran nuclear file yields no resolution
articles.summary: Third UNSC meet on Iran nuclear file yields no resolution The UN Security Council met on Friday for a session since Resolution 2231 technically expired last October, with Russia and China once again forcing a procedural vote before any discussion could begin. The meeting, requested by Bahrain and the Council's five European members--Denmark, France, Greece, Latvia and the UK-- was held under the "Non-Proliferation" agenda item. articles.title: Morocco’s Mohammed VI frigate completes six day New York deployment
articles.summary: Morocco’s Royal Navy frigate Mohammed VI completed a six-day deployment in New York alongside Senegal’s patrol vessel Niani, demonstrating the African navies’ ability to operate far from home and work with international partners, the U.S. Sixth Fleet said. The two vessels took part in the International Naval Review 250, including a July 4 fleet review […] The post Morocco’s Mohammed VI frigate completes six day New York deployment appeared first on HESPRESS English - Morocco News .
